title: Chemistry for Mortals: An atomic look around you
author: Wordsus Team
description: A fascinating 365-day journey through the world of chemistry. From the structure of the atom to the most modern applications of nanotechnology, explained in a simple and entertaining way.

chapters.title: Organic Compounds: The Chemistry of Carbon in Life
chapters.description: We delve into organic chemistry to understand why the carbon atom is so versatile and makes up the basic structure of all living beings.

chapters.title: Alkanes, alkenes and alkynes: hydrocarbon chains
chapters.description: We explore the simplest hydrocarbons, analyzing the differences in reactivity between single, double and triple bonds that support this industry.

chapters.title: The inalterability of gold: a very resistant noble metal
chapters.description: We study why gold does not lose its shine or corrode over the centuries. Know its electronic configuration and its low chemical reactivity.

chapters.title: Stoichiometry: the mathematics behind reactions
chapters.description: Learn how to calculate the exact quantities of reactants and products. This fundamental tool prevents the waste of substances at an industrial level.

chapters.title: Reaction speed: accelerating chemical processes
chapters.description: We analyze chemical kinetics and how factors such as temperature, pressure, concentration and catalysts can accelerate transformations.

chapters.title: Diamond and graphite: the many faces of carbon
chapters.description: Discover the concept of allotropy by comparing two minerals formed only by carbon, but with diametrically opposite properties due to their geometry.

chapters.title: Ideal gases: the laws of Boyle, Charles and Gay-Lussac
chapters.description: We explore how gases respond to changes in pressure, volume and temperature, deducing the historical formulas that explain their vital behavior.

chapters.title: Thermochemistry in action: the reactions that give us heat
chapters.description: We analyze the flow of heat during chemical processes, differentiating exothermic reactions that warm us from endothermic reactions that absorb heat.

chapters.title: Chemistry in the kitchen: Maillard reactions and flavors
chapters.description: We transform your kitchen into a laboratory. Discover the fantastic series of reactions that provide aromas, colors and toasted flavors to all your foods.

chapters.title: Synthetic polymers: the wide universe of plastics
chapters.description: From packaging to textile fibers, we study how small molecules come together to form enormous chains that revolutionized modern life forever.

chapters.title: Chemical balance: reactions that never stop
chapters.description: We understand why many chemical reactions do not complete completely, but rather reach a dynamic state where reactants and products coexist in peace.

chapters.title: Electrolytes in solution: the conduction of electricity
chapters.description: We investigate what happens when certain salts dissolve in pure water, separating into mobile ions that allow the free passage of electric current.

chapters.title: The chemistry of DNA: the molecular code of all beings
chapters.description: We explore the nucleotides and hydrogen bonds that form the famous double helix of DNA, storing humanity's vast genetic information.

chapters.title: Colloids and suspensions: the boundaries of solutions
chapters.description: We analyze those special mixtures that seem homogeneous but are not. From fog to milk, discover the fascinating properties of colloids.

chapters.title: Natural radioactivity: unstable isotopes and their mysteries
chapters.description: We analyze why some atomic nuclei break spontaneously, releasing large doses of energy and invisible radiation.

chapters.title: Avogadro's number and the concept of mole in chemistry
chapters.description: Discover how chemists count atoms and molecules using the concept of the mole, a vital tool that connects visible mass to particles.

chapters.title: Noble gases: the loneliest elements in the table
chapters.description: We explore helium, neon and argon. Learn why these elements are so stable, their zero reactivity and how they are used in modern lighting.

chapters.title: The chemistry of soap: how we clean dirt
chapters.description: We analyze the molecular structure of soaps and detergents, discovering how their molecules manage to trap fat and dissolve it easily in water.

chapters.title: Isomers: molecules with the same formulas and dual forms
chapters.description: Learn why compounds with exactly the same atoms can have completely different physical and chemical properties depending on their spatial shape.

chapters.title: Alkali metals: extreme reactivity when touching water
chapters.description: We focus on sodium and potassium, metals so reactive that they must be stored in oil. Discover why they release so much energy upon aqueous contact.

chapters.title: Crystallography: the perfect geometry hidden in salt
chapters.description: We study how atoms arrange themselves into perfect three-dimensional networks, forming beautiful solid crystals with surprising optical properties.

chapters.title: Fermentation: the chemical miracle behind yeast
chapters.description: We break down the anaerobic metabolic process where sugars are transformed into alcohol and carbon dioxide, essential in baking and beverages.

chapters.title: Halogens: very reactive salt-forming elements
chapters.description: From fluorine to iodine, we analyze this group of non-metals that are highly toxic in their pure state, but essential for life by forming compounds.

chapters.title: Enzymes: the biological catalysts of our body
chapters.description: Discover how these specialized proteins manage to dramatically speed up vital chemical reactions, allowing life to flow without delays.

chapters.title: Distillation: separating liquids using temperature
chapters.description: We explore this ancient laboratory and industrial technique that takes advantage of different boiling points to purify complex liquid mixtures.

chapters.title: The chemistry of chocolate: the delicious cocoa molecule
chapters.description: We analyze theobromine and other complex substances present in chocolate, responsible for its unique flavor and pleasant stimulating effects.

chapters.title: Molecular geometry: the three-dimensional shape of matter
chapters.description: We explain the theory of electron pair repulsion, which determines why molecules adopt unique linear, angular, or tetrahedral shapes.

chapters.title: Alcohols in chemistry: much more than just common drinks
chapters.description: We study the hydroxyl group and properties of alcohols, from dangerous methanol to ethanol and its key use as an industrial solvent.

chapters.title: Batteries and cells: electrochemical cells for daily use
chapters.description: Discover how we store chemical energy to convert it into portable electricity by analyzing the inner workings of lithium-ion cells.

chapters.title: Transition metals: the central block of the table
chapters.description: We delve into metals such as copper, iron and titanium. Understand their multiple oxidation states and why they form such colorful compounds.

chapters.title: Buffer solutions: keeping the pH stabilized
chapters.description: Learn about the vital functioning of buffer systems, specialized chemical mixtures that resist drastic changes in acidity in the blood and industry.

chapters.title: The chemistry of perfumes: esters and synthetic aromas
chapters.description: We discover how organic chemistry creates delicious synthetic fruity and floral fragrances using esters, imitating the smells of nature.

chapters.title: Le Chatelier's principle: balance under real pressure
chapters.description: We analyze how chemical systems react and adjust automatically to counteract any change in temperature, pressure or volume.

chapters.title: Carbohydrates: the sweet fuel of human beings
chapters.description: We explore the chemical structure of sugars and starches, understanding how they store solar energy and act as our body's main source.

chapters.title: Spectroscopy: studying matter through light and color
chapters.description: Learn how modern chemists use different wavelengths of light to identify unknown compounds and reveal their internal structure.

chapters.title: Fireworks: The Colorful Chemistry of Pyrotechnics
chapters.description: We break down the composition of holiday explosives and how salts of different metals generate bright colors when burning at high temperatures.

chapters.title: Chemical titration: the art of measuring concentrations today
chapters.description: We explore the classic volumetric technique used in laboratories to determine the exact amount of a dissolved substance using a standard reagent.

chapters.title: Lipids and fats: long-term energy storage
chapters.description: We analyze the structure of fatty acids, differentiating between saturated and unsaturated fats, and their vital role in the cellular protection of beings.

chapters.title: Surface tension: why insects walk in water
chapters.description: We delve into the cohesion of water molecules on the surface, creating an invisible but strong membrane that defies concepts of gravity.

chapters.title: Amino acids: the building blocks of proteins
chapters.description: Learn about the small organic molecules that link together to form complex protein chains, essential for all the biological processes of our being.

chapters.title: Metal corrosion: the great enemy of infrastructure
chapters.description: We study the oxidation process that deteriorates ships and bridges, analyzing effective chemical methods to prevent the formation of the dreaded rust.

chapters.title: Glass: an amorphous solid with exceptional properties
chapters.description: We discovered the fascinating structure of glass, a fused silica material that cools so quickly that its molecules fail to form a crystal.

chapters.title: Chromatography: separating colors and complex mixtures
chapters.description: Learn how this fundamental analytical technique allows you to separate the individual components of a mixture by making them travel at different phase velocities.

chapters.title: The chemistry of poison: basic toxicology and its antidotes
chapters.description: We analyze how certain molecules can interrupt vital biochemical processes in minimal doses, and how modern science seeks to successfully neutralize them.

chapters.title: Orbital hybridization: how atoms prepare their bonds
chapters.description: We explain the complex model that justifies why carbon can form four identical bonds, rearranging its electrons into hybrid orbitals.

chapters.title: Fluorescence: the magical chemistry that glows at night
chapters.description: Discover the fascinating compounds that absorb invisible light and slowly emit it, creating that greenish glow in the dark without generating heat.

chapters.title: Metal alloys: steel, bronze and new mixtures
chapters.description: We explore how strategic mixing of different metals improves the strength, hardness and durability of the materials that sustain our world.

chapters.title: The chemistry of spicy food: the capsaicin molecule and its heat
chapters.description: We analyze why chili peppers give us that intense sensation of heat and pain, breaking down the chemical structure that tricks our brain.

chapters.title: The nitrogen cycle: the vital nutrient of plants
chapters.description: Learn about the complex chemical journey of nitrogen from the atmosphere to the soil, and how the synthesis of ammonia revolutionized world agriculture.

chapters.title: Acetylsalicylic acid: the science behind aspirin
chapters.description: We unravel the history and molecular structure of the most famous painkiller in the world, discovering how it interacts to soothe our pain.

chapters.title: Rare earths: the secret elements of technology
chapters.description: We study lanthanides, that group of little-known but absolutely essential metals for making powerful magnets and smartphones.

chapters.title: Chemical cosmetics: the formulation of creams and lipsticks
chapters.description: Delve into the chemistry of beauty products, analyzing how emulsions, pigments and waxes combine to care for our skin.

chapters.title: Ozone destruction: the dreaded chlorofluorocarbons
chapters.description: We analyze the chemical reaction in the upper atmosphere that seriously endangered our solar shield, and how science managed to stop the imminent damage.

chapters.title: Reverse osmosis: the great solution to purify water
chapters.description: We understand the chemistry of semipermeable membranes and how pressure forces water molecules to abandon their solutes to purify themselves.

chapters.title: Forensic chemistry: discovering blood with luminol
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating world of investigation focusing on the chemiluminescent reaction that allows forensics to reveal currently hidden evidence.

chapters.title: Heavy metals: the hidden toxicity of lead and mercury
chapters.description: Discover why these dense elements of the periodic table are so dangerous for humans and how they alter our neurological system.

chapters.title: Artificial sweeteners: tricking the sense of taste
chapters.description: We analyze the sweet chemistry of saccharin and aspartame, molecules that our receptors perceive as sugar but that do not provide calories today.

chapters.title: Teflon and polymers: the science of non-stick pots
chapters.description: Explore the strong bonds between carbon and fluorine that create a material so slippery and heat-resistant that nothing ever sticks.

chapters.title: Antioxidants: fighting the terrible free radicals
chapters.description: We understand cellular damage caused by unstable molecular species and how vitamin C or other antioxidants donate electrons to heal us.

chapters.title: Dry ice: the amazing sublimation of solid dioxide
chapters.description: Delve into the physical and chemical process where this icy compound goes directly from solid to gas without leaving a trace of liquid at all.

chapters.title: Metalloids: silicon and modern semiconductors
chapters.description: We study the boundary elements that share properties between metals and non-metals, the fundamental basis for creating all modern electronics.

chapters.title: Hard water: why it is so difficult to generate foam when washing
chapters.description: We analyze the calcium and magnesium salts dissolved in tap water that interfere with the action of soaps and damage pipes with scale.

chapters.title: Adhesives and glues: the chemistry that unites our world
chapters.description: We discover how polymers interact to create mechanical and chemical forces of attraction so strong that they bind impossible materials together.

chapters.title: Bioluminescence: chemical reactions in fireflies
chapters.description: We explore the enzyme luciferase and how nature harnesses oxidation to create warning and attraction lights without generating any heat.

chapters.title: Catalytic converters: cleaning exhaust gases
chapters.description: Learn about the incredible rare metals that transform highly toxic vehicle gases into harmless compounds before you go out into the environment today.

chapters.title: Lactic acid: the intense chemical pain in the muscles
chapters.description: We break down your body's anaerobic metabolic pathway when it requires quick energy, generating a byproduct that produces pain when exercising.

chapters.title: Chlorine and bleach: the intense oxidizing power to clean
chapters.description: We will study the chemistry behind this bleaching and disinfecting compound, analyzing how it destroys bacteria and alters color molecules.

chapters.title: Synthetic fibers: the chemistry of nylon and polyester
chapters.description: We enter the enormous world of laboratory-created textiles, analyzing the strong polymer chains that clothe the modern world today.

chapters.title: Photosynthesis: the reaction that traps light from the universe
chapters.description: We explain the cascade of chemical reactions within plants, transforming carbon dioxide and vital water into sugars and pure oxygen.

chapters.title: Vitamins: essential molecules for our survival
chapters.description: Learn about the complex chemical structure of these organic substances that our body cannot manufacture, but are vital to catalyze reactions.

chapters.title: Kevlar and para-aramids: the chemistry behind armor
chapters.description: We discovered how the powerful chemical bonds of this fiber manage to stop the direct impact of a bullet, being an extremely light material today.

chapters.title: Radioactive decay: half-life and carbon fourteen
chapters.description: We analyze how nuclear chemistry helps archeology determine with extreme precision the age of ancient fossils and relics of humanity.

chapters.title: The chemistry of paper: from hard wood to soft sheets
chapters.description: Explore how harsh industrial processes separate cellulose from lignin, bleaching chemical fibers to create the books you read.

chapters.title: Alloy with memory: the amazing metal called nitinol
chapters.description: We study the intelligent combination of nickel and titanium, capable of recovering its original shape after being deformed just by applying some heat.

chapters.title: Patina and green rust: protecting the Statue of Liberty
chapters.description: We understand the slow atmospheric reactions of copper in the open air, forming a strong green carbonate layer that prevents total corrosion.

chapters.title: Photographic chemistry: developing images with silver
chapters.description: Explore how silver halides react to light to capture moments and the delicate developing process that transformed our view of the world.

chapters.title: Pheromones in action: animal chemical communication
chapters.description: We analyze the volatile molecules that many animals and insects release into the environment to send powerful sexual and vital alert messages.

chapters.title: Silicones and polysiloxanes: very versatile polymers
chapters.description: Discover the unique structure of these materials based on silicon and oxygen bonds, essential for manufacturing modern implants and sealants.

chapters.title: Natural zeolites: molecular rock sponges
chapters.description: We study these fascinating porous minerals capable of trapping ions and specific molecules, being vital to purifying the water we consume.

chapters.title: Baking soda: the chemical leavening of bread
chapters.description: We understand the acid-base reaction that occurs inside your oven, releasing the vital carbon dioxide that puffs up delicious cakes and biscuits.

chapters.title: Hydrogen peroxide: a very effective oxidizing agent
chapters.description: We delve into hydrogen peroxide, an unstable molecule that releases free radicals to disinfect wounds, whiten fabrics and even dye hair.

chapters.title: Petrichor and geosmina: the unmistakable aroma of rain
chapters.description: We break down the curious molecule produced by bacteria in dry soil, responsible for generating that pleasant and nostalgic smell after a storm.

chapters.title: Vulcanization: sulfur hardening rubber today
chapters.description: Learn how heating natural latex together with sulfur creates strong cross bridges, obtaining extremely hard and durable tires.

chapters.title: Cholesterol and steroids: vital carbon rings
chapters.description: We delve into the complex structure of these special lipids, which form cell membranes and synthesize hormones essential for life.

chapters.title: Graphene: the miraculous hexagonal network of pure carbon
chapters.description: We discovered the exceptional properties of this two-dimensional atomic layer, an extremely strong and conductive material that leads the technological future.

chapters.title: Supercritical fluids: when a gas appears liquid
chapters.description: We analyze the state of matter where substances exceed their critical point, being perfect solvents to decaffeinate coffee without using toxins.

chapters.title: Antifreeze: how to prevent water from freezing
chapters.description: Explore how molecules such as ethylene glycol manage to interrupt the hydrogen bonds in water, drastically lowering its solidification point.

chapters.title: Sunscreens: molecules against sunlight
chapters.description: Understand how the chemicals in your sunscreen absorb and scatter UV radiation, saving your skin from serious structural damage.

chapters.title: The chemistry of cyanide: a suffocating and lethal poison
chapters.description: We unravel the dark mechanism of action of this small toxic molecule, which fiercely blocks cellular respiration in all living beings.

chapters.title: Mass spectrometry: weighing individual atoms
chapters.description: Learn about this sophisticated modern analytical technique that ionizes molecules and magnetically deflects them to calculate their weight and determine their purity.

chapters.title: Chemistry of toothpaste: fluoride and our enamel
chapters.description: We analyze how fluoride ions are incorporated directly into tooth structure, forming fluorapatite to resist acids and harsh cavities.

chapters.title: Asphalt and bitumen: the heaviest hydrocarbons today
chapters.description: Discover the densest residue from oil refining, a viscous and black mixture of long carbon chains that today pave our streets.

chapters.title: Fuel cells: the great energy of hydrogen
chapters.description: We study the controlled reaction between hydrogen and oxygen that produces clean and efficient electricity, leaving simple and pure water as the only residue.

chapters.title: The chemistry of inks: colors, resins and media
chapters.description: Explore the complex mix of coloring pigments, organic solvents and thickeners that allow humanity to write and fix ideas on paper.

chapters.title: Chemical preservatives: avoiding natural deterioration
chapters.description: We focus on molecules such as benzoates and sorbates, analyzing how they alter the cellular pH of fungi and bacteria to preserve food.

chapters.title: Lithium Chemistry: Mining White Gold Today
chapters.description: Discover how this light alkaline metal is purified and concentrated from enormous natural brines, being key to all our batteries.

chapters.title: Red and white phosphorus: the chemistry behind matches
chapters.description: We analyze the historical evolution of matches and how friction manages to transform unstable phosphorus to initiate controlled combustion today.

chapters.title: Food coloring: we dye what we consume
chapters.description: Understand the structure of brilliant azo compounds like tartrazine, created in chemical laboratories to make our foods desirable.

chapters.title: Natural alkaloids: the defenses of vegetation
chapters.description: We study these plant nitrogen compounds, from morphine to quinine, that nature created as toxic and we use as medicine.

chapters.title: Heavy water and deuterium: a very useful isotope
chapters.description: We explore the properties of deuterium oxide, a liquid ten percent denser than ordinary water, used to stop neutrons in reactors.

chapters.title: Epoxy resins: high resistance polymerization
chapters.description: Delve into the chemistry of these two-component adhesives. Learn how resin and its hardener react by crossing bonds to create plastics.

chapters.title: Melatonin and tryptophan: the chemistry of good sleep
chapters.description: We analyze the biochemical synthesis of the sleep hormone in our brain and how it regulates the circadian rhythm in the absence of natural light.

chapters.title: Polyurethane foams: chemistry for maximum comfort
chapters.description: We discovered the chemical expansion that generates small bubbles trapped in a strong plastic matrix, creating soft mattresses and thermal insulators.

chapters.title: Tear gas: control irritant molecules
chapters.description: Learn about these aggressive chemical agents that attack the nerve receptors in the mucous membranes of the face, causing intense tearing and strong burning.

chapters.title: The Haber-Bosch process: taking bread out of the air
chapters.description: We analyze the union of atmospheric nitrogen and hydrogen gas under great pressure and heat, generating fertilizers that nourish humanity.

chapters.title: The chemistry of danger: mixing ammonia and chlorine
chapters.description: Discover the chemical reactions that occur when combining common cleaners, releasing toxic chloramine vapors that affect the respiratory system.

chapters.title: Desalination: how we remove salt from seawater
chapters.description: We explore the physical-chemical processes and energy required to purify salt water using membranes, alleviating drinking water shortages.

chapters.title: Why do we cry when cutting an onion in the kitchen?
chapters.description: We analyze the irritating sulfur-based gas that onions release as a natural defense when cut and how it transforms into acid in the eyes.

chapters.title: The power of acetone: dissolving the indissoluble
chapters.description: Delve into the properties of this polar and non-polar organic solvent, capable of dissolving enamels, plastics and resins without leaving any residue today.

chapters.title: Acid rain: when gases damage the ecology
chapters.description: We understand how nitrogen and sulfur oxides mix with atmospheric humidity, generating acid precipitation that corrodes the entire environment.

chapters.title: Endorphins: the natural molecules of pleasure
chapters.description: Learn about the opiate peptides produced by your own brain to relieve physical pain, generate great well-being and maintain our mood.

chapters.title: Boron and borax: an element with multiple uses
chapters.description: We study this exceptional metalloid and how its compounds are used daily to create heat-resistant crystals and cleaning products.

chapters.title: Caramelization: the oxidation of sugar by heat
chapters.description: We break down the complex series of chemical reactions where pure sugar breaks down at high temperatures to create beautiful colors and rich flavors.

chapters.title: The chemistry of cement: hardening step by step
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating hydration reaction, where calcium silicate powder forms strong bonds with water to build great cities.

chapters.title: Tannins: astringency in infusions and wines
chapters.description: We analyze these enormous antioxidant plant molecules that cause that feeling of dryness in your mouth when drinking a delicious glass of red wine or tea.

chapters.title: Absorbent polymers: hidden chemistry in diapers
chapters.description: We discovered how sodium polyacrylate manages to trap water molecules through hydrogen bonds, absorbing hundreds of times its own weight.

chapters.title: Silver sulfide: why our jewelry darkens
chapters.description: Understand the slow reactions between your precious silver objects and traces of environmental sulfur, forming an opaque black layer that is difficult to clean.

chapters.title: Citric acid: the natural acidity of all lemons
chapters.description: We study the organic acid responsible for the sour taste in citrus fruits, its vital role in cellular metabolism and its uses as an effective preservative.

chapters.title: Iodine and the thyroid gland: the chemistry of life
chapters.description: Delve into this halogen essential for the synthesis of hormones, understanding why its deficiency causes serious problems in the human body.

chapters.title: Water chlorination: the treatment that saves populations
chapters.description: Learn how chlorine interacts with harmful microorganisms, destroying their cell membranes to ensure that tap water is drinkable and safe.

chapters.title: Ozone and oxygen: allotropy that protects the planet
chapters.description: We compare the oxygen we breathe with stratospheric ozone, two forms of the same chemical element with radically different functions on Earth.

chapters.title: The chemistry of cheese: coagulating milk step by step
chapters.description: We discovered how rennet enzymes break down casein micelles, altering milk proteins to form very delicious cheeses.

chapters.title: Galvanized: zinc sacrificed to protect metals
chapters.description: We study the electrolytic process where an outer layer of zinc is first deliberately oxidized to save and protect the underlying iron.

chapters.title: Stomach antacids: neutralizing the inner fire
chapters.description: Learn how simple bases like baking soda or magnesium hydroxide react with the hydrochloric acid in your stomach to give you quick relief.

chapters.title: Monosodium glutamate: the fifth flavor of our food
chapters.description: We discovered the molecule responsible for umami, that deep and tasty flavor, analyzing its natural origin and why its synthesis revolutionized cooking.

chapters.title: Dry cleaning: clean your delicate clothes without using water
chapters.description: We explore organic solvents such as perchloroethylene, substances capable of dissolving grease stains without shrinking or causing damage to your delicate fabrics.

chapters.title: Emulsifiers in action: mixing water with oil
chapters.description: Delve into biphasic molecules like egg lecithin, which stabilize microscopic droplets of fat in water to make a rich mayonnaise.

chapters.title: Aluminum extraction: from bauxite to pure metal
chapters.description: We analyze the intensive Hall-Héroult process that uses large electrical currents to isolate this light metal from hard rock oxides.

chapters.title: Phosphorescence: materials glowing in the dark
chapters.description: Understand the chemical difference with fluorescence, analyzing electrons that are trapped and release their accumulated light very slowly at night.

chapters.title: Antiperspirants: aluminum against the sweat of your body
chapters.description: We explore how aluminum salts dissolve with moisture to form a temporary gel that blocks pores, stopping your body sweat.

chapters.title: Penicillin: the molecule that destroys cell walls
chapters.description: Learn about the beta-lactam ring of this vital antibiotic, which prevents harmful bacteria from synthesizing their strong cell walls, healing us.

chapters.title: Hair dyes: the chemistry of color changes
chapters.description: Delve into the permanent reaction where ammonia opens the hair cuticle, allowing new oxidized pigments to replace melanin today.

chapters.title: Hemoglobin: chemical transport within the blood
chapters.description: We study the huge iron-rich protein that reversibly binds oxygen in the lungs to distribute it to all the cells in the body.

chapters.title: Litmus paper: molecules changing color depending on pH
chapters.description: We discover how certain complex organic substances rapidly gain or lose protons, altering their molecular structure and revealing vivid colors.

chapters.title: Carbon monoxide: the terrible invisible chemical killer
chapters.description: We analyze how incomplete combustion generates this fearsome odorless gas, which adheres to the blood much more strongly than the oxygen you breathe.

chapters.title: Ethylene: the invisible gas that ripens our fruits
chapters.description: We discovered the volatile phytohormone that unleashes a cascade of cellular enzymes, breaking down pectins to achieve the sweetness of ripening fruit.

chapters.title: Menthol: the molecule that deceives with false freshness
chapters.description: We analyze how this organic compound activates the cold receptors in our mouth and skin, generating a thermal illusion without lowering the temperature.

chapters.title: Serotonin: the neurotransmitter that regulates your mood
chapters.description: Delve into the fascinating chemical structure of the feel-good hormone, understanding how it modulates our mood, appetite, and daily digestion.

chapters.title: Oxytocin: the powerful chemistry of love and attachments
chapters.description: Explore the critical neuropeptide that fosters social and maternal bonding, analyzing its biochemical release during hugs and genuine affection.

chapters.title: Formaldehyde: the preservation of dead tissues
chapters.description: Learn about the lethal and reactive gas that dissolves in water forming formalin, historically used to stop cell decomposition by crossing proteins.

chapters.title: Propane and butane: hydrocarbons in home cooking
chapters.description: We study the refining of short chain alkanes that liquefy at low pressure, providing great clean caloric energy in stoves and heaters.

chapters.title: Anodized aluminum: oxidize metals to beautify them
chapters.description: We delve into the electrolytic process that thickens the natural oxide layer of aluminum, creating porous surfaces that accept colors and resist.

chapters.title: Degraded lignin: the pleasant smell of old books
chapters.description: We break down how the acid in paper slowly breaks down the wood's polymers, releasing aromatics like benzaldehyde and sweet vanillin today.

chapters.title: Paracetamol: the chemical inhibition of body pain
chapters.description: We analyze the analgesic mechanism of this popular synthetic drug, blocking the synthesis of prostaglandins in the brain to reduce pain and fever.

chapters.title: Cyanoacrylate: the polymer behind quick glue
chapters.description: Discover how a simple drop of water activates the rapid chain reaction of this acrylic resin, creating strong plastic bonds in an almost magical way.

chapters.title: Histamines: the exaggerated allergic chemical reaction today
chapters.description: We understand the nitrogenous molecules that your immune system releases when detecting pollen, causing inflammation, itching and fluids to expel threats.

chapters.title: Trinitrotoluene: the energy released by explosives
chapters.description: We explore the unstable TNT molecule, understanding how the rapid breaking of its nitrogen bonds releases enormous volumes of gas at high pressure.

chapters.title: Uric acid: painful crystals in the joints
chapters.description: We analyze the metabolic byproduct of purines that, when accumulated in the blood due to poor filtration, precipitate forming sharp and painful crystals.

chapters.title: Steviosides: the sweet molecules of plant origin
chapters.description: Delve into the glycosides extracted from the stevia leaf, extremely powerful natural sweeteners that are not metabolized and provide zero calories.

chapters.title: Nitrous oxide: the laughing gas in dentistry today
chapters.description: We study the colorless chemical compound that slows down neurological responses, effectively reducing pain and generating great euphoria in patients.

chapters.title: Cortisol: the chemical hormones that unleash your stress
chapters.description: Learn about the steroid released by the adrenal glands in the face of danger, altering our cellular metabolism to give us immediate escape energy.

chapters.title: Melanin: the pigments that color our skin today
chapters.description: We discover complex macromolecules derived from tyrosine, responsible for absorbing solar radiation to defend your cells from serious damage.

chapters.title: Arsenic: the famous undetectable poison of the Middle Ages
chapters.description: We delved into the toxic metalloid that simulated the symptoms of cholera, blocking cellular enzymes vital for the body's energy production.

chapters.title: Terpenes: the aromatic compounds of pure oils
chapters.description: We explore the organic hydrocarbons present in floral resins, responsible for providing pine, lemon and lavender with characteristic aromas today.

chapters.title: Celluloid and rayon: the first semi-synthetic plastics
chapters.description: We look at the chemical manipulation of plant cellulose using aggressive acids to create soft, high-gloss photographic films and textiles.

chapters.title: Barium sulfate: heavy metals in x-rays today
chapters.description: We study this dense insoluble salt that patients ingest without risk of toxicity, allowing doctors to block X-rays and observe the esophagus.

chapters.title: Ibuprofen: fighting inflammation from its roots
chapters.description: Learn about the structure and powerful mechanism of action of this propionic acid, essential for blocking cellular enzymes that generate inflammation.

chapters.title: Nitrites in meats: preservatives in cured sausages
chapters.description: We break down the salts in sausages, which prevent the deadly botulinum toxin but can form harmful nitrosamines when heated to high temperatures.

chapters.title: Polystyrene: the inflated plastic for single daily use
chapters.description: Delve into this polymer thermally expanded with gases until you achieve an insulating material that unfortunately takes hundreds of years to degrade today.

chapters.title: Chlorophyll: the vital magnesium ring that paints the world
chapters.description: Explore the complex green photoreceptor molecule, analyzing its metallic core that captures solar photons to initiate the synthesis of new sugars.

chapters.title: Dihydroxyacetone: achieve tans without direct sunlight
chapters.description: We discovered the simple carbohydrate that reacts with dead amino acids in the epidermis, creating harmless brown pigments without ultraviolet damage.

chapters.title: Ninhydrin: revealing invisible fingerprints
chapters.description: We analyze the forensic chemical reagent that reacts specifically with the amino acids in human sweat, generating an intense, clear purple color.

chapters.title: Hyaluronic acid: retaining water in our skin
chapters.description: We study this great structural polysaccharide, capable of retaining a thousand times its molecular weight in pure water to keep all of our skin hydrated.

chapters.title: Keratin: the hard protein in our nails and hair
chapters.description: We understand the helical structure rich in the element cellular sulfur, whose strong disulfide bridges give incredible resistance to the hair.

chapters.description: Discover the natural polysaccharide present in fruits that, when combined with sugar and acid, forms a three-dimensional network that thickens all your jellies.

chapters.title: Human pheromones: the myths of chemical attraction
chapters.description: We analyze the complex search for human volatile molecules, debunking trade myths and exploring the true chemical communication between us.

chapters.title: Titanium dioxide: the most used white pigment today
chapters.description: We study the metallic oxide that provides great opacity and a brilliant white to paints, plastics and even cosmetic products such as sunscreens.

chapters.title: Saltpeter and gunpowder: the chemistry of ancient empires
chapters.description: We explore how potassium nitrate reacts violently with carbon and sulfur, creating the first explosive mixture that changed all of history.

chapters.title: Biological dopamine: the circuits of pleasure and desire
chapters.description: Learn about the key neurotransmitter that drives our reward system, motivating our brain through complex and vital biochemical reactions.

chapters.description: We delve into vitamin C, a powerful reducing molecule that gives up its electrons to neutralize the serious damage caused by free radicals today.

chapters.description: We analyze the rapid synthesis of this hormone in extreme situations, accelerating your heart rate and sending great chemical energy directly to the muscles.

chapters.description: We analyze the immense porous network of heat-treated carbon, capable of trapping dangerous toxic molecules in modern purifying filters.

chapters.title: Lead crystal: the dense chemistry of glasses
chapters.description: We study how adding lead oxide to the molten mixture increases the refractive index, creating a heavy material that shines beautifully.

chapters.title: Synthetic neoprene: the rubber of divers today
chapters.description: We discovered the polymerization of chloroprene, generating a resistant thermal insulating material with closed microbubbles to withstand the cold sea.

chapters.title: Cyanobacteria toxins: the danger in lakes
chapters.description: Learn about the fearsome hepatotoxic compounds generated by excessive algae blooms, fueled by agricultural fertilizer spills.

chapters.title: Metallic magnesium: the brightest white light
chapters.description: We explore the immense exothermic reaction of this pure alkaline earth element that generates an intense white flame that is blinding and impossible to extinguish.

chapters.title: Helium in the voice: chemistry of a very light gas
chapters.description: Delve into the physics and chemistry of noble gases. Understand why breathing this inert substance drastically alters the tone of your words.

chapters.title: Pure chloroform: an ancient sleeping agent
chapters.description: We analyze the heavy halogenated compound used by the pioneers of anesthesia and how its liver toxicity forced us to look for new alternatives.

chapters.title: Peracetic acid: dangerous mixtures in the home
chapters.description: We break down the intense and corrosive reaction that occurs when mixing white vinegar with pure hydrogen peroxide, producing serious respiratory damage today.

chapters.title: Physical nucleation: sodas and mint candies
chapters.description: We study the violent and rapid physical degassing of trapped carbon dioxide upon contact with the rough surfaces of a candy.

chapters.title: Trans fats: the hydrogenation of oils
chapters.description: We discovered the industrial method that injects hydrogen gas into liquid lipids to harden them, creating bonds that the body cannot assimilate well.

chapters.title: The chemical olestra: the false lipid without calories
chapters.description: We analyze the enormous sucrose molecule surrounded by fatty acids that digestive enzymes cannot cut, passing intact through the human intestine.

chapters.title: Natural cellulose: the vital indigestible fiber today
chapters.description: Learn about the enormous structural wall of all vegetables, a glucose polymer that humans cannot digest but that cleanses the digestive tract.

chapters.title: Phytic acid and the antinutrients in seeds
chapters.description: We explore the immense molecule capable of chelating important metals in our digestive system, blocking the correct absorption of iron and zinc.

chapters.title: Controlled heat: chemistry of sous vide cooking
chapters.description: Delve into the precise breakdown of tough protein bonds in meats using precise low temperatures to achieve great tenderness and flavor.

chapters.title: Pressure cookers: Gay-Lussac's law at home
chapters.description: We study how the increase in atmospheric pressure raises the boiling point of the liquid, rapidly accelerating the thermal reactions of the stew.

chapters.title: Thawing the ice: chlorides on the streets
chapters.description: We discovered the fascinating freezing point depression of adding salt, disrupting strong hydrogen bonds to melt thick snow.

chapters.title: Cold Compresses: Instant Endothermic Chemistry
chapters.description: We analyze the dissolution of ammonium nitrate in natural water, a rapid reaction that intensely absorbs the surrounding heat to achieve great cold.

chapters.title: Firefighting foam: drowning a big fire today
chapters.description: Learn about the dense aqueous fluorosurfactants that form an immense impenetrable layer on liquid fuels, completely blocking oxidation.

chapters.title: Smoke bombs: slow combustion and refined sugar
chapters.description: We explore the incessant mixture of potassium nitrate with sucrose that gently oxidizes, generating immense and dense columns of opaque gas today.

chapters.title: Optical brighteners: deceiving our vision
chapters.description: Get into the large fluorescent molecules in your detergent that absorb ultraviolet light and emit bluish light, making clothes appear white.

chapters.title: Enzymes in washing: the destruction of stains
chapters.description: We study the strong synthetic lipases and proteases in detergents, painstakingly cutting fats and annoying proteins at low network temperatures.

chapters.title: Laundry softeners: coating fibers with lipids
chapters.description: We discovered how cationic compounds neutralize all the negative charge of the clean fabric, covering it with soft fats that are very pleasant to the touch.

chapters.title: Perfume notes: the chemistry of evaporation
chapters.description: We analyzed the marked differences in chemical volatility between light citrus essential oils and large resinous molecules with a dense background.

chapters.title: Invisible ink: revealing very hidden messages
chapters.description: We explore household acidic substances like lemon that weaken the thin paper locally, carbonizing more quickly when a lot of heat is applied to it.

chapters.title: The molecular future: a year discovering chemistry
chapters.description: We close our great annual cycle by summarizing the immense invisible pillars of matter and glimpsing the astonishing promises of nanotechnology.
